prophecies.title: Born of a Virgin
prophecies.prophecy_text: Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign; Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Mary, a virgin betrothed to Joseph, conceived by the Holy Spirit and gave birth to Jesus in Bethlehem. The angel Gabriel had announced the miraculous conception beforehand. Matthew records this as direct fulfillment of Isaiah's prophecy, noting that the name Immanuel means 'God with us.'
prophecies.modern_status: Central doctrine of Christianity worldwide
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied a virgin would conceive and bear a son called Immanuel. Fulfilled in the birth of Jesus to the virgin Mary.

prophecies.title: Born in Bethlehem
prophecies.prophecy_text: But thou, Bethlehem Ephratah, though thou be little among the thousands of Judah, yet out of thee shall he come forth unto me that is to be ruler in Israel; whose goings forth have been from of old, from everlasting.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea during the reign of Herod the Great. Joseph and Mary had traveled there from Nazareth due to a Roman census decreed by Caesar Augustus. The chief priests and scribes confirmed to Herod that the Messiah was to be born in Bethlehem, citing Micah's prophecy.
prophecies.modern_status: Church of the Nativity stands in Bethlehem today
prophecies.summary: Micah foretold the Messiah would come from the small town of Bethlehem. Jesus was born there when His parents traveled for a Roman census.

prophecies.title: From the Tribe of Judah
prophecies.prophecy_text: The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, nor a lawgiver from between his feet, until Shiloh come; and unto him shall the gathering of the people be.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Both Matthew and Luke record Jesus' genealogy through the tribe of Judah. Luke traces the lineage from Adam through Judah to David and onward to Jesus. The royal line of Israel was preserved through the tribe of Judah, and Jesus was recognized as belonging to this tribe by His contemporaries.
prophecies.modern_status: Genealogical records preserved in the Gospels
prophecies.summary: Jacob prophesied the sceptre would remain with the tribe of Judah until Shiloh (the Messiah) came. Jesus was born of the tribe of Judah.

prophecies.title: From the Line of David
prophecies.prophecy_text: Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will raise unto David a righteous Branch, and a King shall reign and prosper, and shall execute judgment and justice in the earth.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Matthew opens his Gospel with 'The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.' Both Joseph's legal lineage and Mary's bloodline trace back to King David. This Davidic descent was publicly acknowledged and never disputed by Jesus' contemporaries or opponents.
prophecies.modern_status: Davidic lineage documented in two Gospel genealogies
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah prophesied God would raise a righteous Branch from David's line to reign as King. Jesus' genealogy in both Matthew and Luke confirms His Davidic descent.

prophecies.title: Ministry in Galilee
prophecies.prophecy_text: Nevertheless the dimness shall not be such as was in her vexation, when at the first he lightly afflicted the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, and afterward did more grievously afflict her by the way of the sea, beyond Jordan, in Galilee of the nations. The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light: they that dwell in the land of the shadow of death, upon them hath the light shined.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Jesus moved from Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali by the Sea of Galilee. He conducted the majority of His public ministry in Galilee, teaching in synagogues, healing the sick, and performing miracles. Matthew explicitly cites Isaiah 9:1-2 as fulfilled by Jesus' Galilean ministry.
prophecies.modern_status: Capernaum archaeological site visited by millions annually
prophecies.summary: Isaiah foretold a great light shining in Galilee of the nations. Jesus based His ministry in Capernaum, Galilee, bringing the light of His teaching to that region.

prophecies.title: Triumphal Entry on a Donkey
prophecies.prophecy_text: Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion; shout, O daughter of Jerusalem: behold, thy King cometh unto thee: he is just, and having salvation; lowly, and riding upon an ass, and upon a colt the foal of an ass.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: On what is now called Palm Sunday, Jesus sent two disciples to fetch a donkey and its colt from a nearby village. He rode the colt into Jerusalem while crowds spread their garments and palm branches on the road, crying 'Hosanna to the Son of David.' All four Gospels record this event, and Matthew explicitly notes it fulfilled Zechariah's prophecy.
prophecies.modern_status: Commemorated annually on Palm Sunday worldwide
prophecies.summary: Zechariah prophesied Israel's King would enter Jerusalem riding on a donkey. Jesus rode a colt into Jerusalem to the acclaim of crowds waving palm branches.

prophecies.title: Betrayed by a Friend
prophecies.prophecy_text: Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, which did eat of my bread, hath lifted up his heel against me.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve apostles who had traveled, eaten, and ministered alongside Jesus for three years, betrayed Him to the chief priests. Jesus identified the betrayer at the Last Supper by dipping bread and giving it to Judas. John records that Jesus explicitly referenced Psalm 41:9 in identifying His betrayer.
prophecies.modern_status: Judas' betrayal is one of the most recognized events in history
prophecies.summary: The Psalmist foretold betrayal by a close, trusted friend who shared his bread. Judas Iscariot, one of Jesus' own disciples, betrayed Him after sharing the Last Supper.

prophecies.title: Sold for Thirty Pieces of Silver
prophecies.prophecy_text: And I said unto them, If ye think good, give me my price; and if not, forbear. So they weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Judas Iscariot went to the chief priests and asked what they would give him to deliver Jesus to them. They agreed on exactly thirty pieces of silver, the price of a slave under Mosaic law. This precise amount, prophesied over 500 years earlier, was the sum for which the Messiah was betrayed.
prophecies.modern_status: 'Thirty pieces of silver' remains a universal idiom for betrayal
prophecies.summary: Zechariah prophesied the price of thirty pieces of silver. Judas received exactly thirty silver coins from the chief priests for betraying Jesus.

prophecies.title: Silver Thrown in the Temple
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the LORD said unto me, Cast it unto the potter: a goodly price that I was prised at of them. And I took the thirty pieces of silver, and cast them to the potter in the house of the LORD.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Overcome with remorse after Jesus was condemned, Judas returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests in the temple, casting down the coins before them. The priests, deeming the money blood money unfit for the treasury, used it to purchase the potter's field as a burial place for strangers. Matthew records this as fulfillment of prophecy.
prophecies.modern_status: The traditional site of Akeldama (Field of Blood) is in the Hinnom Valley, Jerusalem
prophecies.summary: Zechariah foretold the thirty silver pieces would be cast to the potter in the house of the Lord. Judas threw the coins in the temple and the money bought a potter's field.

prophecies.title: Silent Before Accusers
prophecies.prophecy_text: He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he opened not his mouth: he is brought as a lamb to the slaughter, and as a sheep before her shearers is dumb, so he openeth not his mouth.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: When brought before the Sanhedrin, Herod, and Pontius Pilate, Jesus remained largely silent in the face of false accusations. Pilate marveled that He answered nothing, even when multiple witnesses testified against Him. This composure under extreme duress fulfilled Isaiah's image of a lamb silent before its shearers.
prophecies.modern_status: Jesus' silence before Pilate is depicted in art and literature across centuries
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied the servant would not open his mouth before his accusers, like a lamb led to slaughter. Jesus remained silent during His trials before the Sanhedrin and Pilate.

prophecies.title: Pierced Hands and Feet
prophecies.prophecy_text: For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Jesus was crucified by Roman soldiers who drove nails through His hands and feet to affix Him to the cross. After His resurrection, He showed the nail wounds to Thomas, who had doubted. This prophecy is remarkable because it was written centuries before crucifixion was invented as a method of execution by the Persians and later adopted by Rome.
prophecies.modern_status: Archaeological evidence of Roman crucifixion confirmed by the Jehohanan heel bone discovery (1968)
prophecies.summary: David described the piercing of hands and feet centuries before crucifixion existed. Jesus' hands and feet were pierced by nails at His crucifixion.

prophecies.title: Crucified with Criminals
prophecies.prophecy_text: Therefore will I divide him a portion with the great, and he shall divide the spoil with the strong; because he hath poured out his soul unto death: and he was numbered with the transgressors; and he bare the sin of many, and made intercession for the transgressors.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Jesus was crucified between two criminals on Golgotha. Mark records that this fulfilled Isaiah's prophecy that He would be 'numbered with the transgressors.' One of the criminals repented and Jesus promised him paradise. Jesus also interceded for His executioners, praying 'Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.'
prophecies.modern_status: The three crosses remain Christianity's most recognized symbol
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied the servant would be numbered with transgressors. Jesus was crucified between two criminals at Golgotha.

prophecies.title: Garments Divided and Lots Cast
prophecies.prophecy_text: They part my garments among them, and cast lots upon my vesture.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Roman soldiers at the crucifixion divided Jesus' outer garments into four parts, one for each soldier. His inner tunic was seamless, woven in one piece, so rather than tear it they cast lots to determine who would receive it. John explicitly notes this fulfilled the Psalm 22 prophecy, detailing both the dividing and the lot-casting.
prophecies.modern_status: Several churches claim to possess relics of the Holy Robe
prophecies.summary: David foretold garments being divided and lots cast for clothing. Roman soldiers divided Jesus' garments and cast lots for His seamless tunic.

prophecies.title: Given Vinegar to Drink
prophecies.prophecy_text: They gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: While on the cross, Jesus said 'I thirst.' A sponge was filled with vinegar (sour wine), placed on a hyssop branch, and raised to His lips. Earlier He had also been offered wine mixed with gall (myrrh), which He tasted but refused to drink. Both elements of the prophecy were fulfilled in detail.
prophecies.modern_status: Detail confirmed by Roman crucifixion practices documented by historians
prophecies.summary: The Psalmist prophesied being given gall and vinegar to drink. On the cross, Jesus was offered wine mixed with gall and later given vinegar on a sponge.

prophecies.title: No Bones Broken
prophecies.prophecy_text: He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Roman soldiers customarily broke the legs of crucified victims to hasten death before the Sabbath. They broke the legs of the two criminals crucified alongside Jesus, but when they came to Jesus, they found He was already dead and did not break His legs. John records this as fulfillment of Scripture that not a bone would be broken.
prophecies.modern_status: Also parallels the Passover lamb requirement of Exodus 12:46
prophecies.summary: Scripture foretold none of the Messiah's bones would be broken. Though soldiers broke the legs of those crucified with Him, Jesus' bones were left intact.

prophecies.title: Side Pierced
prophecies.prophecy_text: And I will pour upon the house of David, and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the spirit of grace and of supplications: and they shall look upon me whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn for him, as one mourneth for his only son.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: A Roman soldier thrust a spear into Jesus' side to confirm His death, and immediately blood and water flowed out. John, who witnessed it, records this as fulfillment of Zechariah's prophecy. The flow of blood and water is medically consistent with a piercing of the pericardial sac, indicating Jesus had already died.
prophecies.modern_status: Medical analyses confirm the blood-and-water detail as physiologically accurate for post-mortem lance wound
prophecies.summary: Zechariah prophesied they would look upon the one they pierced. A Roman soldier pierced Jesus' side with a spear, producing blood and water.

prophecies.title: Buried in a Rich Man's Tomb
prophecies.prophecy_text: And he made his grave with the wicked, and with the rich in his death; because he had done no violence, neither was any deceit in his mouth.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Joseph of Arimathea, a wealthy member of the Sanhedrin and secret follower of Jesus, requested the body from Pilate. He wrapped it in clean linen and placed it in his own new tomb hewn out of rock. This fulfilled the dual prophecy: Jesus made His grave 'with the wicked' in His death by crucifixion, and 'with the rich' in His burial.
prophecies.modern_status: The Church of the Holy Sepulchre and Garden Tomb are both venerated as the burial site
prophecies.summary: Isaiah foretold the servant would be buried with the rich despite dying with the wicked. The wealthy Joseph of Arimathea placed Jesus in his own new tomb.

prophecies.title: Resurrection from the Dead
prophecies.prophecy_text: For thou wilt not leave my soul in hell; neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: On the third day after His crucifixion, Jesus rose bodily from the tomb. The stone was rolled away, and the tomb was found empty by women who had come to anoint the body. Over the following forty days, He appeared to over five hundred witnesses. Peter cited Psalm 16:10 at Pentecost, arguing David spoke prophetically of the Messiah's resurrection.
prophecies.modern_status: The resurrection is the central claim of Christianity, celebrated on Easter worldwide
prophecies.summary: David prophesied God would not let His Holy One see corruption in the grave. Jesus rose from the dead on the third day, as Peter proclaimed at Pentecost.

prophecies.title: Ascension to Heaven
prophecies.prophecy_text: Thou hast ascended on high, thou hast led captivity captive: thou hast received gifts for men; yea, for the rebellious also, that the LORD God might dwell among them.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Forty days after His resurrection, Jesus led His disciples to the Mount of Olives near Bethany. After commissioning them to be His witnesses to the ends of the earth, He was taken up bodily into heaven while they watched. A cloud received Him out of their sight, and two angels appeared confirming He would return in like manner.
prophecies.modern_status: The Chapel of the Ascension on the Mount of Olives marks the traditional site
prophecies.summary: The Psalmist foretold an ascension on high, leading captivity captive. Jesus ascended bodily into heaven from the Mount of Olives forty days after His resurrection.

prophecies.title: Seventy Weeks of Daniel
prophecies.prophecy_text: Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people and upon thy holy city, to finish the transgression, and to make an end of sins, and to make reconciliation for iniquity, and to bring in everlasting righteousness, and to seal up the vision and prophecy, and to anoint the most Holy. Know therefore and understand, that from the going forth of the commandment to restore and to build Jerusalem unto the Messiah the Prince shall be seven weeks, and threescore and two weeks.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Daniel's prophecy of 69 weeks (483 prophetic years) from the decree to rebuild Jerusalem to the Messiah the Prince has been calculated by scholars to align precisely with the time of Jesus' public ministry. The decree of Artaxerxes in 457 BC to rebuild Jerusalem, counted forward 483 years, arrives at approximately AD 27, the start of Jesus' ministry. The prophecy also foretold that after 69 weeks the Messiah would be 'cut off.'
prophecies.modern_status: One of the most mathematically analyzed prophecies in biblical scholarship
prophecies.summary: Daniel prophesied a precise timeline of seventy weeks of years pointing to the Messiah. The calculation aligns with the period of Jesus' ministry and crucifixion.

prophecies.title: The Suffering Servant
prophecies.prophecy_text: He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief: and we hid as it were our faces from him; he was despised, and we esteemed him not. Surely he hath borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows: yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted. But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Isaiah 53 describes a suffering servant who bears the sins of many, is wounded and bruised, oppressed and afflicted, yet does not open his mouth. Jesus fulfilled this through His passion: He was despised, rejected, scourged, wounded, and crucified as a substitutionary sacrifice. The Ethiopian eunuch was reading this passage when Philip explained it referred to Jesus.
prophecies.modern_status: Isaiah 53 is the most frequently cited Old Testament passage in messianic discussions
prophecies.summary: Isaiah described a suffering servant despised, rejected, and wounded for others' transgressions. Jesus fulfilled this through His suffering, crucifixion, and substitutionary death.

prophecies.title: Preceded by a Messenger
prophecies.prophecy_text: Behold, I will send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me: and the Lord, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple, even the messenger of the covenant, whom ye delight in: behold, he shall come, saith the LORD of hosts.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: John the Baptist came preaching in the wilderness of Judea, calling for repentance and baptizing in the Jordan River. He declared himself the voice crying in the wilderness, preparing the way of the Lord. Mark's Gospel opens by identifying John as the fulfillment of Malachi's prophecy, the messenger sent ahead to prepare the way for the Messiah.
prophecies.modern_status: John the Baptist is recognized as the forerunner across Christian traditions
prophecies.summary: Malachi prophesied a messenger would prepare the way before the Lord. John the Baptist fulfilled this role by preaching repentance before Jesus' public ministry.

prophecies.title: Rejected by His Own People
prophecies.prophecy_text: He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief: and we hid as it were our faces from him; he was despised, and we esteemed him not.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Despite performing miracles and teaching with authority, Jesus was rejected by the religious leaders and much of the populace of Israel. John records that 'He came unto his own, and his own received him not.' The Sanhedrin condemned Him, the crowds chose Barabbas, and He was delivered to the Romans for execution.
prophecies.modern_status: The rejection of the Messiah by His own is a central theme in Christian theology
prophecies.summary: Isaiah foretold the servant would be despised and rejected by his own people. Jesus was rejected by the Israelite leaders and delivered to Rome for crucifixion.

prophecies.title: Darkness Over the Land
prophecies.prophecy_text: And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Lord GOD, that I will cause the sun to go down at noon, and I will darken the earth in the clear day.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: During Jesus' crucifixion, from the sixth hour (noon) to the ninth hour (3 PM), darkness fell over the whole land. This three-hour darkness at midday could not have been a solar eclipse, as Passover occurs during a full moon. Roman historian Thallus and Phlegon of Tralles both reference an unusual darkness in this period.
prophecies.modern_status: Extra-biblical references to this darkness survive in fragments of Roman historical writing
prophecies.summary: Amos prophesied God would darken the earth at noon. During Jesus' crucifixion, darkness covered the land from noon to 3 PM.

prophecies.title: Seed of the Woman
prophecies.prophecy_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Known as the protoevangelium (first gospel), this prophecy speaks of one born of a woman who would crush the serpent's head while suffering a wound to His heel. Christians understand this as fulfilled in Jesus, born of a woman (Mary) without a human father, who defeated Satan through His death and resurrection. The 'bruised heel' represents the crucifixion; the 'crushed head' represents ultimate victory over evil.
prophecies.modern_status: Recognized as the first messianic prophecy in Scripture
prophecies.summary: God told the serpent that the seed of the woman would crush his head. Jesus, born of a woman, defeated Satan through His death and resurrection.

prophecies.title: Flight to Egypt
prophecies.prophecy_text: When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: An angel warned Joseph in a dream to flee to Egypt with Mary and the infant Jesus to escape Herod's plot to kill the child. The family remained in Egypt until Herod's death, then returned to Israel. Matthew explicitly states this fulfilled Hosea's words: 'Out of Egypt have I called my son.'
prophecies.modern_status: The Coptic Church traces its origins to the Holy Family's time in Egypt
prophecies.summary: Hosea spoke of God calling His son out of Egypt. The infant Jesus was taken to Egypt to escape Herod, then called back after Herod's death.

prophecies.title: Massacre of the Innocents
prophecies.prophecy_text: Thus saith the LORD; A voice was heard in Ramah, lamentation, and bitter weeping; Rahel weeping for her children refused to be comforted for her children, because they were not.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: When the Magi did not return to report Jesus' location, Herod the Great ordered the slaughter of all male children two years old and under in Bethlehem and its surrounding areas. Matthew records the weeping and lamentation that followed and explicitly cites Jeremiah 31:15 as fulfilled by this tragic event. Rachel's tomb was traditionally located near Bethlehem.
prophecies.modern_status: Commemorated on December 28 as the Feast of the Holy Innocents
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah foretold Rachel weeping for her children in Ramah. Herod's massacre of infants in Bethlehem fulfilled this prophecy of bitter lamentation.

prophecies.title: Ministry of Miracles
prophecies.prophecy_text: Then the eyes of the blind shall be opened, and the ears of the deaf shall be unstopped. Then shall the lame man leap as an hart, and the tongue of the dumb sing: for in the wilderness shall waters break out, and streams in the desert.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: When John the Baptist sent disciples to ask if Jesus was the expected one, Jesus pointed to His miracles as evidence: 'The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.' The Gospels record numerous healings of the blind, deaf, lame, and mute throughout Jesus' ministry.
prophecies.modern_status: The miracles of Jesus remain among the most studied events in religious history
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied the blind, deaf, lame, and mute would be healed. Jesus pointed to His miracles of healing as evidence of His messianic identity.

prophecies.title: Called Out of Egypt
prophecies.prophecy_text: When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: After Herod's death, an angel appeared to Joseph in Egypt instructing him to return to the land of Israel with the child and His mother. Joseph obeyed and brought the family back, settling in Nazareth of Galilee. Matthew identifies this return from Egypt as fulfillment of Hosea's prophecy about God's son being called out of Egypt.
prophecies.modern_status: The route of the Holy Family through Egypt is traced by Coptic Christian tradition
prophecies.summary: Hosea declared God called His son out of Egypt. After Herod died, Jesus was brought back from Egypt to Israel, fulfilling this prophetic pattern.

prophecies.title: A Light to the Gentiles
prophecies.prophecy_text: I the LORD have called thee in righteousness, and will hold thine hand, and will keep thee, and give thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: When the infant Jesus was presented at the temple, the aged Simeon declared Him 'a light to lighten the Gentiles, and the glory of thy people Israel.' Jesus' ministry extended beyond Israel to Samaritans, Romans, and others. After His resurrection, He commissioned His followers to take the gospel to all nations. Christianity spread throughout the Roman Empire and beyond.
prophecies.modern_status: The gospel of Yeshua has spread to every nation on earth, fulfilling the promise that He would be a light to all peoples
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied the servant would be a light to the Gentiles. Simeon recognized infant Jesus as this light, and the gospel spread to all nations.

prophecies.title: Fall of Babylon
prophecies.prophecy_text: And Babylon, the glory of kingdoms, the beauty of the Chaldees' excellency, shall be as when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah. It shall never be inhabited, neither shall it be dwelt in from generation to generation: neither shall the Arabian pitch tent there; neither shall the shepherds make their fold there.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: In 539 BC, the Persian army under Cyrus the Great conquered Babylon by diverting the Euphrates River and entering through the dry riverbed under the city walls. The city gradually declined under Persian, Greek, and Parthian rule. By the first century AD, Babylon was largely abandoned. Today its ruins lie desolate in modern Iraq, never having been rebuilt as a functioning city.
prophecies.modern_status: Ruins remain uninhabited in Iraq near Hillah; UNESCO World Heritage Site since 2019
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied Babylon would fall and never be reinhabited. Persia conquered it in 539 BC and its ruins remain desolate today.

prophecies.title: Destruction of Tyre
prophecies.prophecy_text: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I am against thee, O Tyrus, and will cause many nations to come up against thee, as the sea causeth his waves to come up. And they shall destroy the walls of Tyrus, and break down her towers: I will also scrape her dust from her, and make her like the top of a rock.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Nebuchadnezzar besieged mainland Tyre for 13 years (585-572 BC), destroying it. The inhabitants fled to an island half a mile offshore. In 332 BC, Alexander the Great built a causeway to the island using the rubble of old Tyre, literally scraping the city bare to the rock. He then conquered the island city. Multiple nations attacked Tyre over centuries exactly as prophesied.
prophecies.modern_status: Ancient Tyre is an archaeological site; the causeway Alexander built is now a permanent land bridge in modern Lebanon
prophecies.summary: Ezekiel prophesied Tyre would be destroyed by many nations and scraped bare like a rock. Nebuchadnezzar and Alexander the Great fulfilled this in succession.

prophecies.title: Fall of Nineveh
prophecies.prophecy_text: The LORD is slow to anger, and great in power, and will not at all acquit the wicked: the LORD hath his way in the whirlwind and in the storm, and the clouds are the dust of his feet.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Nineveh, the great capital of the Assyrian Empire, fell in 612 BC to a coalition of Babylonians, Medes, and Scythians. Nahum had prophesied the city would be destroyed by flooding, and ancient accounts confirm the Tigris River flooded and destroyed part of the city walls, allowing the attackers to breach the defenses. The city was so thoroughly destroyed that its location was lost for over two thousand years until rediscovered in the 1840s.
prophecies.modern_status: Ruins near modern Mosul, Iraq; site was damaged by ISIS in 2015 but is being restored
prophecies.summary: Nahum prophesied Nineveh's total destruction, partly by flood. The Assyrian capital fell in 612 BC when river flooding breached its walls.

prophecies.title: Egypt Diminished Among Nations
prophecies.prophecy_text: It shall be the basest of the kingdoms; neither shall it exalt itself any more above the nations: for I will diminish them, that they shall no more rule over the nations.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: After millennia as one of the ancient world's greatest powers, Egypt was conquered successively by Persia (525 BC), Greece (332 BC), and Rome (30 BC). It has never regained its former status as a dominant world empire. For over two thousand years, Egypt has been ruled by foreign powers or remained a regional rather than global power, exactly as Ezekiel prophesied.
prophecies.modern_status: Egypt is a regional power but has never returned to its ancient imperial status
prophecies.summary: Ezekiel prophesied Egypt would become the basest of kingdoms and never again rule over nations. Egypt has not regained its ancient imperial dominance.

prophecies.title: Rise of the Persian Empire
prophecies.prophecy_text: The ram which thou sawest having two horns are the kings of Media and Persia.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Daniel's vision of a ram with two horns was explicitly identified as the Medo-Persian Empire. The two horns represented Media and Persia, with the higher horn (Persia) coming up last. Cyrus the Great united the Medes and Persians and established the largest empire the world had yet seen, conquering Babylon in 539 BC and ruling from India to Ethiopia.
prophecies.modern_status: The Achaemenid Persian Empire is well-documented in archaeological and historical records
prophecies.summary: Daniel prophesied the rise of the Medo-Persian Empire symbolized by a two-horned ram. Cyrus united Media and Persia into the ancient world's largest empire.

prophecies.title: Rise of Greece
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the rough goat is the king of Grecia: and the great horn that is between his eyes is the first king.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Daniel saw a goat with a prominent horn charging from the west and shattering the ram (Persia). This was explicitly identified as Greece, with the great horn being its first king. Alexander the Great of Macedon launched his campaign against Persia in 334 BC, defeating Darius III at Issus and Gaugamela, and conquered the entire Persian Empire in just over a decade.
prophecies.modern_status: Alexander's conquests are among the best-documented events of the ancient world
prophecies.summary: Daniel prophesied Greece (a goat) would defeat Persia (a ram), with its first great king leading the charge. Alexander the Great conquered the Persian Empire.

prophecies.title: Alexander's Empire Divided into Four
prophecies.prophecy_text: Therefore the he goat waxed very great: and when he was strong, the great horn was broken; and for it came up four notable ones toward the four winds of heaven.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Alexander the Great died suddenly in Babylon in 323 BC at age 32, at the height of his power. After the Wars of the Diadochi, his empire was divided among four of his generals: Cassander took Macedonia and Greece, Lysimachus took Thrace and Asia Minor, Seleucus took Syria and the East, and Ptolemy took Egypt. This four-way division matched Daniel's vision precisely.
prophecies.modern_status: The four successor kingdoms are well-attested in Hellenistic historical records
prophecies.summary: Daniel foresaw the great horn (first king) breaking and four kingdoms arising. After Alexander's death, his empire split among four generals.

prophecies.title: Rise of the Roman Empire
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the fourth kingdom shall be strong as iron: forasmuch as iron breaketh in pieces and subdueth all things: and as iron that breaketh all these, shall it break in pieces and bruise.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Daniel interpreted Nebuchadnezzar's dream of a great statue whose legs were iron as a fourth kingdom that would crush all others. Following Babylon (gold), Persia (silver), and Greece (bronze), Rome arose as the iron kingdom. Rome conquered the Mediterranean world with its legendary military discipline, crushing the Greek successor states and establishing an empire that endured for centuries.
prophecies.modern_status: The Roman Empire's legacy permeates Western law, language, and governance to this day
prophecies.summary: Daniel prophesied a fourth kingdom strong as iron that would break and subdue all others. The Roman Empire fulfilled this as the dominant power after Greece.

prophecies.title: Desolation of Edom
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the house of Jacob shall be a fire, and the house of Joseph a flame, and the house of Esau for stubble, and they shall kindle in them, and devour them; and there shall not be any remaining of the house of Esau; for the LORD hath spoken it.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: The Edomites, descendants of Esau, were gradually displaced from their homeland by the Nabataeans beginning in the 4th century BC. They were later absorbed into Judean territory as Idumeans during the Hasmonean period. After the destruction of Jerusalem in AD 70, the Edomite/Idumean identity ceased to exist entirely. No people group today claims Edomite descent.
prophecies.modern_status: The Edomite people no longer exist; their former territory in southern Jordan is largely desolate
prophecies.summary: Obadiah prophesied there would be no survivor from the house of Esau (Edom). The Edomites were displaced, absorbed, and eventually ceased to exist as a people.

prophecies.title: Philistia Destroyed
prophecies.prophecy_text: For Gaza shall be forsaken, and Ashkelon a desolation: they shall drive out Ashdod at the noon day, and Ekron shall be rooted up. Woe unto the inhabitants of the sea coast, the nation of the Cherethites! the word of the LORD is against you; O Canaan, the land of the Philistines, I will even destroy thee, that there shall be no inhabitant.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: The Philistine cities were conquered by Babylon under Nebuchadnezzar in the early 6th century BC. The Philistines as a distinct ethnic group gradually disappeared from history after the Babylonian and Persian conquests. Alexander the Great besieged and destroyed Gaza in 332 BC, killing or enslaving the population. The Philistine civilization never recovered.
prophecies.modern_status: The Philistines ceased to exist as a people; the ancient cities are archaeological sites
prophecies.summary: Zephaniah prophesied the destruction of the Philistine cities and the annihilation of the Philistine nation. Babylon and later Alexander destroyed them.

prophecies.title: Judgment of Moab
prophecies.prophecy_text: The burden of Moab. Because in the night Ar of Moab is laid waste, and brought to silence; because in the night Kir of Moab is laid waste, and brought to silence.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Moab was conquered by Babylon in the 6th century BC and its cities were destroyed. The Moabite people were gradually absorbed into the Nabataean and Arab populations. By the first century AD, Moab as a distinct nation had ceased to exist. The prophet's detailed description of specific cities being laid waste has been confirmed by archaeological surveys of the region.
prophecies.modern_status: The Moabite civilization no longer exists; ruins are found in modern Jordan
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied the devastation of Moab and its cities. Moab was conquered and its people absorbed, ceasing to exist as a nation.

prophecies.title: Destruction of Damascus
prophecies.prophecy_text: The burden of Damascus. Behold, Damascus is taken away from being a city, and it shall be a ruinous heap.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Damascus was conquered and destroyed by the Assyrian king Tiglath-Pileser III in 732 BC, ending the Aramean kingdom of Damascus. The city was reduced to an Assyrian province. Though Damascus was later rebuilt and has been continuously inhabited, the Aramean kingdom it represented was permanently destroyed, fulfilling Isaiah's prophecy of its removal as a sovereign city-state.
prophecies.modern_status: Damascus is the capital of modern Syria and one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied Damascus would cease being a city and become a ruinous heap. Assyria conquered and destroyed the Aramean kingdom of Damascus in 732 BC.

prophecies.title: Cyrus Named by Prophecy
prophecies.prophecy_text: That saith of Cyrus, He is my shepherd, and shall perform all my pleasure: even saying to Jerusalem, Thou shalt be built; and to the temple, Thy foundation shall be laid. Thus saith the LORD to his anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have holden, to subdue nations before him.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Isaiah named Cyrus as God's chosen instrument approximately 150 years before Cyrus was born. Cyrus the Great of Persia conquered Babylon in 539 BC and issued a decree allowing the Israelite exiles to return to Jerusalem and rebuild their temple. The Cyrus Cylinder confirms his policy of restoring peoples to their homelands and rebuilding their temples.
prophecies.modern_status: The Cyrus Cylinder in the British Museum confirms Cyrus's policy of religious restoration
prophecies.summary: Isaiah named Cyrus by name 150 years before his birth, prophesying he would rebuild Jerusalem. Cyrus conquered Babylon and decreed the temple's restoration.

prophecies.title: Babylon's Walls and Gates Fall
prophecies.prophecy_text: Thus saith the LORD to his anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have holden, to subdue nations before him; and I will loose the loins of kings, to open before him the two leaved gates; and the gates shall not be shut.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: According to Herodotus and the Nabonidus Chronicle, Cyrus's forces diverted the Euphrates River and entered Babylon through the river gates, which had been left open during a festival. The city's massive walls, considered impregnable, were bypassed entirely. Babylon fell with minimal resistance, and King Belshazzar was slain that very night.
prophecies.modern_status: The fall of Babylon is confirmed by multiple ancient Near Eastern sources
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied that gates would be opened before Cyrus and not shut. Babylon's river gates were left open, allowing the Persian army to enter unopposed.

prophecies.title: Sidon Preserved
prophecies.prophecy_text: And say, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I am against thee, O Zidon; and I will be glorified in the midst of thee: and they shall know that I am the LORD, when I shall have executed judgments in her, and shall be sanctified in her. For I will send into her pestilence, and blood into her streets.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Unlike Tyre, which was prophesied to be destroyed and never rebuilt, Sidon was prophesied to suffer bloodshed and pestilence but not total destruction. Throughout history, Sidon has endured numerous conquests and disasters but has survived and been continuously inhabited. It suffered under Persia, was sacked by the Persians in 351 BC with great loss of life, yet the city endured.
prophecies.modern_status: Sidon (modern Saida, Lebanon) remains an inhabited city of over 250,000 people
prophecies.summary: Ezekiel prophesied suffering for Sidon but not its total destruction, unlike Tyre. Sidon has endured many conquests but remains inhabited to this day.

prophecies.title: Egypt Invaded by Babylon
prophecies.prophecy_text: Thus saith the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel; Behold, I will send and take Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon, my servant, and will set his throne upon these stones that I have hid; and he shall spread his royal pavilion over them.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Nebuchadnezzar invaded Egypt in 568/567 BC, as recorded in a fragmentary Babylonian chronicle. Jeremiah had specifically prophesied this invasion, even detailing that Nebuchadnezzar would set up his throne at Tahpanhes in Egypt. Archaeological evidence from Tell Defenneh (ancient Tahpanhes) and Babylonian records confirm the campaign against Pharaoh Amasis.
prophecies.modern_status: Babylonian chronicle fragments confirm the Egyptian campaign
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah prophesied Nebuchadnezzar would invade Egypt and set his throne at Tahpanhes. Babylonian records confirm the invasion occurred around 568 BC.

prophecies.title: Media Attacks Babylon
prophecies.prophecy_text: Behold, I will stir up the Medes against them, which shall not regard silver; and as for gold, they shall not delight in it.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Isaiah specifically identified the Medes as the instrument of Babylon's destruction, writing approximately 150 years before the event. The Medes, allied with the Persians under Cyrus, attacked and conquered Babylon in 539 BC. The prophecy noted they could not be bought off with silver or gold, indicating a determined military conquest rather than a negotiated submission.
prophecies.modern_status: The Medo-Persian conquest of Babylon is one of the best-documented events of the ancient world
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied the Medes would attack Babylon 150 years before it happened. The Medes and Persians conquered Babylon in 539 BC.

prophecies.title: Petra Left Desolate
prophecies.prophecy_text: Thy terribleness hath deceived thee, and the pride of thine heart, O thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rock, that holdest the height of the hill: though thou shouldest make thy nest as high as the eagle, I will bring thee down from thence, saith the LORD. Also Edom shall be a desolation: every one that goeth by it shall be astonished, and shall hiss at all the plagues thereof.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Petra, the rock-carved capital of the Edomites and later the Nabataeans, was one of the ancient world's most impressive cities, carved into cliff faces in a nearly inaccessible canyon. After the Roman annexation of the Nabataean kingdom in AD 106 and a devastating earthquake in AD 363, Petra was gradually abandoned. It became a forgotten city until its rediscovery by Johann Ludwig Burckhardt in 1812.
prophecies.modern_status: Petra is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World, but remains uninhabited
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah prophesied the dwellers in the rock clefts would be brought down and Edom left desolate. Petra was abandoned and lost for centuries.

prophecies.title: Tyre Never Rebuilt
prophecies.prophecy_text: And they shall lay thy stones and thy timber and thy dust in the midst of the water. And I will make thee like the top of a rock: thou shalt be a place to spread nets upon; thou shalt be built no more: for I the LORD have spoken it, saith the Lord GOD.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Ancient Tyre's stones, timber, and dust were literally thrown into the sea when Alexander the Great built his causeway in 332 BC. The original Phoenician city of Tyre was never rebuilt to its former glory. The site of ancient mainland Tyre became a bare rock used by fishermen to spread and dry their nets, precisely as Ezekiel described. Modern Sur (Tyre) is a different settlement.
prophecies.modern_status: The ancient city site is bare rock; fishermen have historically used it for spreading nets
prophecies.summary: Ezekiel prophesied Tyre's rubble would be cast into the sea and the site would become a place for spreading nets. Alexander fulfilled this literally.

prophecies.title: Judgment of Ammon
prophecies.prophecy_text: Concerning the Ammonites, thus saith the LORD; Hath Israel no sons? hath he no heir? why then doth their king inherit Gad, and his people dwell in his cities? Therefore, behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will cause an alarm of war to be heard in Rabbah of the Ammonites; and it shall be a desolate heap.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: The Ammonites were conquered by Nebuchadnezzar around 582 BC. Their capital Rabbah (modern Amman, Jordan) was attacked and their territory absorbed. The Ammonites as a distinct people gradually disappeared through conquest and assimilation during the Persian and Hellenistic periods. Ptolemy II Philadelphus rebuilt Rabbah as Philadelphia, erasing its Ammonite identity.
prophecies.modern_status: The Ammonites no longer exist as a people; their capital Rabbah is now Amman, Jordan's capital
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah prophesied war and desolation for the Ammonites and their capital Rabbah. Babylon conquered them and the Ammonite nation eventually ceased to exist.

prophecies.title: Babylonian Exile Prophesied
prophecies.prophecy_text: And this whole land shall be a desolation, and an astonishment; and these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Nebuchadnezzar besieged Jerusalem three times (605, 597, and 586 BC), ultimately destroying the city and Solomon's temple in 586 BC. The people of Judah were deported to Babylon in successive waves. This exile fundamentally transformed Israelite worship, shifting from temple-centered practice to synagogue-based study of Scripture.
prophecies.modern_status: The Babylonian exile is confirmed by the Lachish Letters and Babylonian Chronicles
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah prophesied Judah would serve Babylon and the land would be desolate. Nebuchadnezzar destroyed Jerusalem and deported the people in 586 BC.

prophecies.title: Seventy-Year Captivity
prophecies.prophecy_text: For thus saith the LORD, That after seventy years be accomplished at Babylon I will visit you, and perform my good word toward you, in causing you to return to this place.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: The seventy years of captivity, counted from the first deportation in 605 BC to Cyrus's decree in 538 BC (or from the temple's destruction in 586 BC to its rededication in 516 BC), were fulfilled precisely. Daniel himself, reading Jeremiah's prophecy, understood the seventy years were nearing completion and prayed for restoration.
prophecies.modern_status: The precise duration of the exile is confirmed by multiple historical chronologies
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah specified the Babylonian captivity would last exactly seventy years. The exile lasted from 605 to 538 BC (or 586 to 516 BC for the temple).

prophecies.title: Return from Exile
prophecies.prophecy_text: That saith of Cyrus, He is my shepherd, and shall perform all my pleasure: even saying to Jerusalem, Thou shalt be built; and to the temple, Thy foundation shall be laid.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: In 538 BC, Cyrus the Great issued a decree permitting the Israelite exiles to return to Jerusalem and rebuild their temple. Zerubbabel led the first wave of approximately 42,000 returnees. Ezra led a second wave, and Nehemiah later returned to rebuild the city walls. The second temple was completed in 516 BC, exactly seventy years after the first temple's destruction.
prophecies.modern_status: The Cyrus Cylinder confirms the Persian policy of returning exiled peoples
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied Cyrus would decree Jerusalem and the temple rebuilt. Cyrus issued the decree in 538 BC and the exiles returned.

prophecies.title: Ten Tribes Scattered
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the LORD shall scatter thee among all people, from the one end of the earth even unto the other; and there thou shalt serve other gods, which neither thou nor thy fathers have known, even wood and stone.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: The northern kingdom of Israel, comprising ten tribes, was conquered by Assyria in 722 BC. The Assyrian policy of deportation and population transfer scattered the Israelites across the Assyrian Empire. These exiles were resettled in Halah, Gozan, and the cities of the Medes. They became known as the 'lost tribes' as they were dispersed among the nations.
prophecies.modern_status: Communities worldwide (Beta Israel, Bnei Menashe, Pashtun) claim descent from the lost tribes
prophecies.summary: Moses prophesied scattering among all nations. The ten northern tribes were deported by Assyria in 722 BC and dispersed across the ancient world.

prophecies.title: Judah Exiled to Babylon
prophecies.prophecy_text: And I will deliver Zedekiah king of Judah, and his servants, and the people, and such as are left in this city from the pestilence, from the sword, and from the famine, into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Jeremiah specifically prophesied that Judah would be delivered into Nebuchadnezzar's hand. In 586 BC, after an 18-month siege, Jerusalem fell. King Zedekiah was captured trying to flee, his sons were killed before his eyes, and he was blinded and taken in chains to Babylon. The temple treasures were plundered and the city burned, exactly as Jeremiah warned.
prophecies.modern_status: Babylonian siege of Jerusalem confirmed by Lachish Letters and archaeological evidence
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah prophesied Judah would be delivered to Nebuchadnezzar. Jerusalem fell in 586 BC and the people were exiled to Babylon.

prophecies.title: Temple Destroyed in AD 70
prophecies.prophecy_text: And Jesus said unto them, See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: In AD 70, Roman legions under Titus besieged and conquered Jerusalem after a brutal five-month campaign. The temple was set ablaze, and soldiers dismantled it stone by stone to recover the gold that had melted between the stones. Josephus records that over a million people perished. The destruction was so complete that the exact location of the temple's Holy of Holies is still debated.
prophecies.modern_status: The Western Wall (retaining wall) remains; the Arch of Titus in Rome depicts the temple treasures being carried away
prophecies.summary: Jesus prophesied not one stone of the temple would remain upon another. The Romans destroyed the temple in AD 70, dismantling it completely.

prophecies.title: Scattering After AD 70
prophecies.prophecy_text: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: After the destruction of Jerusalem in AD 70 and the Bar Kokhba revolt in AD 135, the Israelites were scattered across the Roman Empire and eventually throughout the world. The Romans renamed Jerusalem Aelia Capitolina and banned Israelites from the city. For nearly 1,900 years, Jerusalem was controlled by successive Gentile powers: Rome, Byzantium, the Arab Caliphates, Crusaders, Mamluks, Ottomans, and the British.
prophecies.modern_status: The diaspora scattered Israelite communities across every continent — the Deuteronomy 28 curses serve as the scriptural identifier for where those scattered Israelites are today
prophecies.summary: Jesus prophesied the people would be led captive into all nations and Jerusalem trodden by Gentiles. After AD 70, a worldwide scattering occurred. The Deuteronomy 28 curses identify the true scattered Israelites.

prophecies.title: Israel a Byword Among Nations
prophecies.prophecy_text: And thou shalt become an astonishment, a proverb, and a byword, among all nations whither the LORD shall lead thee.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: This curse is part of the full Deuteronomy 28 pattern and must be evaluated alongside the other markers: slavery by ships (v.68), scattering among all nations (v.64), serving enemies under a yoke of iron (v.48), and becoming the tail and not the head (v.43-44). The people who fulfill this curse of becoming 'a byword among all nations' are the same people who match ALL the Deuteronomy 28 curses — not selectively applied to one group while ignoring the other markers. The true Israelites, scattered among the nations, have been subject to scorn, persecution, and proverb wherever the LORD led them.
prophecies.modern_status: This curse must be read in context with all Deuteronomy 28 markers — the true Israelites are identified by the full pattern of curses, not selectively
prophecies.summary: Moses prophesied Israel would become a byword and astonishment among all nations. This curse is part of the full Deuteronomy 28 identification pattern for the true children of Israel.

prophecies.title: Preservation as a People
prophecies.prophecy_text: Thus saith the LORD, which giveth the sun for a light by day, and the ordinances of the moon and of the stars for a light by night, which divideth the sea when the waves thereof roar; The LORD of hosts is his name: If those ordinances depart from before me, saith the LORD, then the seed of Israel also shall cease from being a nation before me for ever.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Despite conquests by Babylon, Persia, Greece, and Rome, despite the destruction of their temple and homeland, and despite nearly two millennia of exile and persecution across dozens of nations, the seed of Israel has not ceased. No other ancient nation (Babylon, Assyria, Philistia, Moab, Edom) has endured through comparable dispersion. The identity of the preserved seed is a scripturally significant question — Deuteronomy 28 provides the identifying markers: slavery by ships (v.68), scattering among all nations (v.64), oppression (v.33), becoming a byword (v.37), and becoming the tail and not the head (v.43-44). The true children of Israel are identified not by modern religious or ethnic labels but by whether they match the curses Moses prophesied.
prophecies.modern_status: God promised the seed of Israel would never cease — the Deuteronomy 28 curses serve as the biblical identifier for the true children of Israel
prophecies.summary: Jeremiah prophesied the seed of Israel would never cease as a nation. God has preserved them, and the Deuteronomy 28 curses provide the scriptural markers for identifying the true Israelites.

prophecies.title: Jerusalem Besieged by Many Nations
prophecies.prophecy_text: Behold, I will make Jerusalem a cup of trembling unto all the people round about, when they shall be in the siege both against Judah and against Jerusalem.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Jerusalem has been besieged, attacked, or conquered more than 50 times throughout recorded history. It has been captured by Babylonians, Persians, Greeks, Ptolemies, Seleucids, Romans, Arabs, Crusaders, Mamluks, Ottomans, and the British. The city has been a source of contention and trembling for surrounding nations throughout the centuries, exactly as Zechariah prophesied.
prophecies.modern_status: Jerusalem remains politically contested — the ultimate prophetic fulfillment (Zechariah 14) involves the Messiah's return and divine intervention, not current geopolitics
prophecies.summary: Zechariah prophesied Jerusalem would be a cup of trembling to surrounding nations. The city has been besieged over 50 times. The ultimate fulfillment awaits the Messiah's return.

prophecies.title: Land Left Desolate
prophecies.prophecy_text: And I will bring the land into desolation: and your enemies which dwell therein shall be astonished at it.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: After the Roman destruction in AD 70 and the suppression of the Bar Kokhba revolt in AD 135, the land of Israel entered centuries of decline. Mark Twain, visiting in 1867, described it as a desolate country with hardly a tree or shrub. The land remained largely barren and sparsely populated for centuries, astonishing visitors who knew its biblical history of productivity.
prophecies.modern_status: The land was barren for centuries as prophesied; modern agricultural development has altered the landscape, but the prophesied spiritual restoration under the Messiah (Ezekiel 36:24-28) has not yet occurred
prophecies.summary: Moses prophesied the land would become desolate, astonishing even Israel's enemies. For centuries after AD 70, the land was barren. The prophesied spiritual restoration under the Messiah remains unfulfilled.

prophecies.title: Regathering of Israel Prophesied
prophecies.prophecy_text: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will take the children of Israel from among the heathen, whither they be gone, and will gather them on every side, and bring them into their own land: And I will make them one nation in the land upon the mountains of Israel; and one king shall be king to them all... Neither shall they defile themselves any more with their idols... but I will save them... and will cleanse them: so shall they be my people, and I will be their God. And David my servant shall be king over them; and they all shall have one shepherd.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: This prophecy describes a two-stage restoration: first, a physical regathering from the nations, and second, a spiritual cleansing where the regathered people follow God's servant David (the Messiah) as their shepherd. The full prophecy requires that the regathered nation be cleansed, follow one shepherd, and walk in God's judgments. The modern state of Israel, established in 1948 through the political Zionist movement and UN Partition Plan, represents a political gathering but does not fulfill the spiritual conditions: the nation does not acknowledge Yeshua (Jesus) as Messiah, and no Davidic king reigns. Ezekiel 36:26-27 further specifies God would give them 'a new heart' and 'a new spirit' — this spiritual transformation has not occurred. Additionally, Revelation 2:9 and 3:9 warn of those who 'say they are Jews, and are not,' raising the scriptural question of whether the modern political state represents the true children of Israel whom Ezekiel describes. The identifying marks of the true Israelites according to Deuteronomy 28 — sold into slavery by ships, scattered among all nations, oppressed, made the tail and not the head — do not align with the establishment of a powerful nation-state through political negotiation.
prophecies.modern_status: The modern state of Israel (est. 1948) represents a political regathering but the spiritual conditions of Ezekiel 37:23-28 remain unfulfilled — no Davidic king, no spiritual cleansing, no acknowledgment of Yeshua as Messiah
prophecies.summary: Ezekiel prophesied God would gather the children of Israel from the nations, cleanse them, and set David's servant as their king. The full prophecy includes spiritual restoration under the Messiah, which has not yet been fulfilled.

prophecies.title: Synagogue of Satan — False Claims to Israelite Identity
prophecies.prophecy_text: I know thy works, and tribulation, and poverty, (but thou art rich) and I know the blasphemy of them which say they are Jews, and are not, but are the synagogue of Satan... Behold, I will make them of the synagogue of Satan, which say they are Jews, and are not, but do lie; behold, I will make them to come and worship before thy feet, and to know that I have loved thee.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Christ warned of a group who would claim to be Jews (Israelites) but are not, calling them the 'synagogue of Satan.' This warning, given twice in Revelation to the churches of Smyrna and Philadelphia, indicates an ongoing deception regarding Israelite identity. Scripture provides clear markers for identifying the true children of Israel through the curses of Deuteronomy 28: they would be taken captive into all nations (v.64), brought into bondage by ships (v.68), sold as slaves (v.68), oppressed and crushed continually (v.33), become a byword and proverb among nations (v.37), the stranger would rise above them and they would become the tail (v.43-44), and they would serve their enemies under a yoke of iron (v.48). Notably, Genesis 10:3 identifies Ashkenaz as a descendant of Japheth through Gomer — not of Shem through Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. The majority population of the modern state of Israel are Ashkenazi Jews of European origin. The Deuteronomy 28 curses — particularly slavery by ships, being scattered as the lowest among nations, and serving under an iron yoke — historically align with the experience of the transatlantic slave trade and its descendants, not with European populations who established a nation-state through political negotiation with Western powers.
prophecies.modern_status: The question of true Israelite identity remains scripturally significant — Deuteronomy 28 curses serve as the biblical identifier, and Revelation warns that some claiming the identity are not who they say they are
prophecies.summary: Christ twice warned of those who 'say they are Jews, and are not, but are the synagogue of Satan.' Scripture provides identifying marks of the true Israelites through the Deuteronomy 28 curses, which describe slavery, scattering, and oppression — not political statehood.

prophecies.title: Covenant with Abraham
prophecies.prophecy_text: Now the LORD had said unto Abram, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father's house, unto a land that I will shew thee: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt be a blessing: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: From Abraham descended the nation of Israel, through whom came the Scriptures, the prophets, and ultimately the Messiah. Galatians 3:16 makes clear: 'Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.' The promise that all families of the earth would be blessed through Abraham is fulfilled through Yeshua (Jesus), the seed of Abraham, through whom salvation is offered to all nations. Galatians 3:8 confirms: 'the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham.'
prophecies.modern_status: The blessing to all nations is fulfilled through the gospel of Yeshua the Messiah, the seed of Abraham (Galatians 3:16)
prophecies.summary: God promised Abraham that all families of the earth would be blessed through his seed. Galatians 3:16 identifies that seed as Christ — through whom salvation is offered to all nations.

prophecies.title: Blessings and Curses of Deuteronomy 28
prophecies.prophecy_text: And it shall come to pass, if thou shalt hearken diligently unto the voice of the LORD thy God, to observe and to do all his commandments which I command thee this day, that the LORD thy God will set thee on high above all nations of the earth.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: The blessings and curses of Deuteronomy 28 have been remarkably demonstrated throughout Israelite history. During periods of faithfulness (under David, Solomon), the nation prospered. During periods of disobedience, every curse was fulfilled: siege, famine, exile, persecution, scattering among nations, and becoming a byword. Beyond historical record, the Deuteronomy 28 curses serve as the scriptural identification markers for the true children of Israel today. The curses describe a people who would be: taken captive by ships into slavery (v.68), scattered among all nations (v.64), oppressed and crushed continually (v.33), made the tail and not the head (v.43-44), serve their enemies in hunger, thirst, nakedness, and under a yoke of iron (v.48), and become a byword among all nations (v.37). These markers identify a specific historical experience — not political statehood, but slavery, scattering, and oppression.
prophecies.modern_status: The Deuteronomy 28 curses serve as the biblical identification markers for the true children of Israel — describing slavery by ships, scattering, oppression, and becoming a byword among nations
prophecies.summary: Moses outlined specific blessings for obedience and curses for disobedience. The curses also serve as the scriptural identification markers for the true children of Israel.

prophecies.title: Jacob's Blessing Over the Twelve Tribes
prophecies.prophecy_text: And Jacob called unto his sons, and said, Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you that which shall befall you in the last days.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Jacob's deathbed blessings over his twelve sons prophetically described the character and destiny of each tribe. Judah received the royal sceptre (fulfilled in David's dynasty), Joseph received the double portion through Ephraim and Manasseh, Benjamin was described as a ravenous wolf (fulfilled in Saul and the warriors of Benjamin), and each tribe's history aligned with Jacob's words.
prophecies.modern_status: The tribal identities shaped Israelite history for over a millennium
prophecies.summary: Jacob prophesied the future of each of his twelve sons' tribes on his deathbed. The history of each tribe reflected his prophetic blessings.

prophecies.title: The Kingdom Divided
prophecies.prophecy_text: And Ahijah caught the new garment that was on him, and rent it in twelve pieces: And he said to Jeroboam, Take thee ten pieces: for thus saith the LORD, the God of Israel, Behold, I will rend the kingdom out of the hand of Solomon, and will give ten tribes to thee.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: After Solomon's death in approximately 930 BC, his son Rehoboam refused to lighten the heavy taxes and forced labor. The ten northern tribes revolted under Jeroboam, exactly as the prophet Ahijah had foretold by tearing a garment into twelve pieces and giving Jeroboam ten. Only Judah and Benjamin remained loyal to the house of David, forming the southern kingdom.
prophecies.modern_status: The divided monarchy is confirmed by archaeological evidence from both kingdoms
prophecies.summary: The prophet Ahijah foretold the kingdom would split, with ten tribes given to Jeroboam. After Solomon's death, the kingdom divided exactly as prophesied.

prophecies.title: Samaria Falls to Assyria
prophecies.prophecy_text: Samaria shall become desolate; for she hath rebelled against her God: they shall fall by the sword: their infants shall be dashed in pieces, and their women with child shall be ripped up.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Samaria, the capital of the northern kingdom of Israel, fell to the Assyrian king Shalmaneser V after a three-year siege, completed by Sargon II in 722 BC. The Assyrians were notorious for their brutal treatment of conquered peoples. The population was deported and replaced with peoples from other conquered lands, ending the northern kingdom permanently.
prophecies.modern_status: The site of ancient Samaria (Sebastia) contains ruins from multiple periods of occupation
prophecies.summary: Hosea prophesied Samaria would become desolate for rebelling against God. Assyria conquered Samaria in 722 BC and deported its population.

prophecies.title: Judah Outlasts Israel
prophecies.prophecy_text: Within threescore and five years shall Ephraim be broken, that it be not a people.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Isaiah prophesied that Ephraim (the northern kingdom) would be broken within 65 years, while Judah would continue. The northern kingdom fell to Assyria in 722 BC, approximately 13 years after this prophecy. Assyria then resettled foreign peoples in the territory, and within 65 years of the prophecy (by approximately 670 BC), the Israelite identity in the north was completely dissolved through intermarriage and assimilation.
prophecies.modern_status: The southern kingdom of Judah continued for over 130 years after the north fell
prophecies.summary: Isaiah prophesied Ephraim would cease to be a people within 65 years while implying Judah would continue. The northern kingdom fell while Judah persisted.

prophecies.title: Suffering for Disobedience
prophecies.prophecy_text: But it shall come to pass, if thou wilt not hearken unto the voice of the LORD thy God, to observe to do all his commandments and his statutes which I command thee this day; that all these curses shall come upon thee, and overtake thee.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: The detailed curses of Deuteronomy 28 describe siege conditions (fulfilled at Jerusalem in 586 BC and AD 70), exile among nations, persecution, fear, and oppression by foreign powers. Josephus records that during the Roman siege, conditions in Jerusalem precisely matched Moses' descriptions: starvation so severe that people consumed their own children, as Moses had warned.
prophecies.modern_status: The curses of Deuteronomy 28 are studied as a prophetic roadmap of Israelite suffering
prophecies.summary: Moses detailed specific curses for disobedience including siege, famine, exile, and persecution. These have been fulfilled repeatedly throughout Israelite history.

prophecies.title: Moses' Blessing Over the Tribes
prophecies.prophecy_text: And this is the blessing, wherewith Moses the man of God blessed the children of Israel before his death.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Moses blessed each tribe before his death, prophesying their future. Joseph's tribes received abundant agricultural blessings (fulfilled in the fertile territory of Ephraim and Manasseh), Zebulun and Issachar were associated with maritime prosperity, Gad received territory east of the Jordan, and Levi was set apart for priestly service without a territorial inheritance, all as prophesied.
prophecies.modern_status: The tribal territorial allotments matched the character of Moses' blessings
prophecies.summary: Moses prophetically blessed each tribe before his death, describing their future territories and roles. The tribal histories corresponded to his blessings.

prophecies.title: The Great Flood
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the LORD said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: God warned Noah of a coming flood to destroy all life, giving him instructions to build an ark. The flood came as promised, destroying all living things not aboard the ark. Noah, his family, and representative animals survived. Flood accounts exist in over 200 cultures worldwide, including the Sumerian Eridu Genesis, the Babylonian Epic of Gilgamesh, and traditions from China, India, Greece, and the Americas.
prophecies.modern_status: Over 200 flood traditions exist across world cultures; geological evidence of massive ancient flooding is studied
prophecies.summary: God warned Noah He would destroy the earth by flood. The flood came as prophesied, and flood accounts from over 200 cultures corroborate the event.

prophecies.title: Tower of Babel and Scattering of Nations
prophecies.prophecy_text: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another's speech. So the LORD scattered them abroad from thence upon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the city.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: God confounded the single language of humanity at Babel, scattering the peoples across the earth. This event explains the diversity of languages and the dispersion of peoples from Mesopotamia. The remains of ziggurats (stepped temple towers) in ancient Sumer are consistent with the narrative. Linguistic studies confirm that language families diverged from ancient Near Eastern origins.
prophecies.modern_status: Over 7,000 languages exist today; ancient ziggurats remain in Iraq
prophecies.summary: God declared He would confound humanity's language and scatter them from Babel. The diversity of world languages and dispersal of peoples followed.

prophecies.title: Destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah
prophecies.prophecy_text: Because the cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is very grievous; I will go down now, and see whether they have done altogether according to the cry of it, which is come unto me; and if not, I will know.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: God rained brimstone and fire upon Sodom and Gomorrah, destroying the cities and all their inhabitants. Only Lot and his two daughters escaped. The destruction was so complete that the cities' location was lost for millennia. Recent excavations at Tall el-Hammam in Jordan have uncovered evidence of a massive high-temperature destruction event consistent with the biblical account, including trinitite-like spherules and melted pottery.
prophecies.modern_status: Tall el-Hammam excavations provide archaeological evidence of a catastrophic destruction event
prophecies.summary: God announced the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah for their wickedness. The cities were destroyed by fire and brimstone, with archaeological evidence at Tall el-Hammam.

prophecies.title: The Ten Plagues of Egypt
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the LORD said unto Moses, See, I have made thee a god to Pharaoh: and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet. Thou shalt speak all that I command thee: and Aaron thy brother shall speak unto Pharaoh, that he send the children of Israel out of his land.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: God sent ten plagues upon Egypt exactly as He declared through Moses: water turned to blood, frogs, lice, flies, livestock pestilence, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, and the death of the firstborn. Each plague was announced beforehand and came to pass precisely. The Ipuwer Papyrus, an ancient Egyptian document, describes conditions remarkably similar to the plagues, including the Nile turning to blood and widespread devastation.
prophecies.modern_status: The Ipuwer Papyrus in the Leiden Museum contains parallels to the plague narrative
prophecies.summary: God declared ten specific plagues would strike Egypt through Moses. Each plague came exactly as announced, with parallels found in the Ipuwer Papyrus.

prophecies.title: Parting of the Red Sea
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the LORD said unto Moses, Wherefore criest thou unto me? speak unto the children of Israel, that they go forward: But lift thou up thy rod, and stretch out thine hand over the sea, and divide it: and the children of Israel shall go on dry ground through the midst of the sea.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: God told Moses to stretch his rod over the sea, and the waters would divide. Moses obeyed, and a strong east wind drove the sea back all night, creating a path of dry ground with walls of water on either side. The Israelites crossed safely, but when the Egyptians pursued, the waters returned and destroyed Pharaoh's army. This event became the defining act of deliverance in Israelite history.
prophecies.modern_status: Commemorated annually in the Passover celebration; the crossing site is debated among scholars
prophecies.summary: God told Moses to stretch his rod over the sea and it would divide. The waters parted, Israel crossed on dry ground, and Pharaoh's army was destroyed.

prophecies.title: Walls of Jericho Fall
prophecies.prophecy_text: And the LORD said unto Joshua, See, I have given into thine hand Jericho, and the king thereof, and the mighty men of valour. And ye shall compass the city, all ye men of war, and go round about the city once. Thus shalt thou do six days.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: God instructed Joshua to march around Jericho for seven days, with seven priests blowing seven trumpets. On the seventh day, they marched seven times and shouted, and the walls fell flat. Archaeological excavations by John Garstang and Kathleen Kenyon at Tell es-Sultan found evidence of collapsed walls falling outward and a destruction layer with burned remains, consistent with the biblical account.
prophecies.modern_status: Excavations at Tell es-Sultan (Jericho) show collapsed walls and a burn layer from the Late Bronze Age
prophecies.summary: God told Joshua Jericho's walls would fall after seven days of marching. The walls collapsed, and archaeological evidence shows walls that fell outward.

prophecies.title: The Sun Stands Still
prophecies.prophecy_text: Then spake Joshua to the LORD in the day when the LORD delivered up the Amorites before the children of Israel, and he said in the sight of Israel, Sun, stand thou still upon Gibeon; and thou, Moon, in the valley of Ajalon.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Joshua commanded the sun to stand still over Gibeon during battle against the Amorites, and God honored his request. The sun did not go down for about a whole day, giving Israel time to complete their victory. The text notes there was never a day like it before or after. Some researchers have noted that ancient cultures worldwide, including Chinese and Aztec traditions, record anomalous long days or long nights consistent with this event.
prophecies.modern_status: Various ancient cultures record anomalous solar events; debated among astronomers and historians
prophecies.summary: Joshua commanded the sun to stand still, and God extended the day for Israel's victory. Ancient records from multiple cultures reference an anomalous long day.

prophecies.title: Elijah Versus the Prophets of Baal
prophecies.prophecy_text: And call ye on the name of your gods, and I will call on the name of the LORD: and the God that answereth by fire, let him be God. And all the people answered and said, It is well spoken.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: Elijah challenged 450 prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel, proposing that the true God would answer by fire. The prophets of Baal called on their god from morning to evening with no response. Elijah then repaired the altar of the Lord, drenched the sacrifice and wood with water three times, and prayed. Fire fell from heaven, consuming the sacrifice, wood, stones, dust, and the water in the trench.
prophecies.modern_status: Mount Carmel remains a pilgrimage site; the Carmelite order takes its name from this location
prophecies.summary: Elijah declared the true God would answer by fire on Mount Carmel. Fire fell from heaven and consumed the water-drenched sacrifice.

prophecies.title: Jonah and the Repentance of Nineveh
prophecies.prophecy_text: Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and cry against it; for their wickedness is come up before me.
prophecies.fulfillment_text: God sent Jonah to pronounce judgment on Nineveh, declaring the city would be overthrown in forty days. After Jonah's reluctant preaching, the entire city from the king to the lowest citizen repented in sackcloth and ashes. God relented from the declared destruction. This mass repentance is unique in Scripture and extended Nineveh's existence for over a century before it finally fell in 612 BC.
